channel.title: SEA Food & Agriculture Dispatch
channel.description: Food and agriculture news from Southeast Asia

item.title: ADB commits $200m to Mekong Delta agroecology facility with cooperative-led disbursement
item.description: The Asian Development Bank has announced a $200 million facility targeting agroecological transition across Vietnam, Cambodia, and Laos, with capital to be disbursed through farmer-led cooperative structures over a three-year horizon. The facility is the bank's first dedicated agroecology vehicle in the region and explicitly prioritises smallholder equity and just and regenerative supply chain outcomes. Initial deployments will focus on rice and freshwater aquaculture systems.

item.title: Aquaspark rebuilds Asia-Pacific due diligence framework around smallholder network depth
item.description: Impact investor Aquaspark has updated its Asia-Pacific aquaculture due diligence framework, explicitly screening portfolio companies on the depth of their smallholder producer network relationships and adoption of agroecological sourcing commitments. The revised framework takes effect for all new investments from Q2 2026 and is being shared publicly for peer reference. Aquaspark cites a need to move beyond certification-based screening toward systems-level assessment.

item.title: Indonesian cooperative federation unites 2,400 smallholder rice farmers under single agroecology charter
item.description: A newly formed federation of cooperatives in Central Java has brought 2,400 smallholder rice farmers under a single agroecology charter, enabling direct-to-market contracts with three regional food processors. The federation took eighteen months to establish legal standing and is being watched as a model for adjacent provinces. Member farmers report eighteen percent price premiums over spot market rates on first contracts.

item.title: Unilever commits to sourcing fifty percent of Southeast Asian agricultural inputs from agroecological systems by 2030
item.description: Unilever has announced a commitment to source fifty percent of its Southeast Asian agricultural inputs from agroecological systems by 2030, with milestone targets and third-party verification to be detailed in Q3 2026. The commitment covers palm oil, tea, and soy supply chains across Indonesia, Malaysia, and Vietnam, and is framed explicitly as a shift beyond certification-based sustainability.

item.title: Thai Rice Department opens consultation on subsidy reform favouring regenerative cultivation
item.description: Thailand's Rice Department has opened public consultation on proposed subsidy reforms that would redirect funding toward regenerative and low-input rice production. The consultation runs through 15 May 2026 and is the first formal policy signal from the department since the 2024 change in agricultural leadership. Consultation documents reference smallholder resilience as a primary design goal.

item.title: Philippines begins drafting national agroecology strategy with regional consultation workshops
item.description: The Philippine Department of Agriculture has begun drafting a national agroecology strategy, with consultation workshops planned across all regions through July 2026. If adopted, the strategy would be the first national framework of its kind in ASEAN and is being developed in coordination with smallholder farmer associations.

item.title: Vietnamese shrimp exporter Minh Phu pivots supply chain to regenerative aquaculture
item.description: Vietnamese shrimp exporter Minh Phu has announced a commitment to source one hundred percent of product from regenerative aquaculture operations by 2028, with interim milestones tied to smallholder partnership expansion in the Mekong Delta. The commitment is the largest of its kind in the region and has been positioned as a response to tightening European import standards.

item.title: Cambodian federation of smallholder cooperatives wins regional sustainability award
item.description: A federation of eighteen smallholder cooperatives in northern Cambodia has won regional recognition for an integrated agroecology and market access model that has increased average member income by thirty-four percent over three years. The federation plans to expand its model to Ratanakiri and Mondulkiri provinces in the coming year.

item.title: GFI APAC publishes consumer research showing twelve percent decline in Southeast Asian alt-protein interest
item.description: The Good Food Institute APAC has published research suggesting Southeast Asian consumer interest in alternative proteins has declined twelve percent year-over-year, with price and taste cited as primary barriers. The findings are being read by some investors as a challenge to recent investment theses in the regional alternative protein sector.

item.title: Nestle expands agroecology supplier programme in Thailand to 2,800 smallholders
item.description: Nestle has expanded its Thai agroecology supplier programme to include an additional 2,800 smallholder farmers across rice and vegetable supply, with technical training and premium pricing for certified output. The expansion brings programme coverage to over seven thousand farmers and is being framed as a response to climate volatility in the region.

item.title: Southeast Asian ESG disclosure requirements tighten for listed agribusiness
item.description: Several Southeast Asian financial regulators have moved toward tighter ESG disclosure requirements for publicly-listed agribusiness companies, with new reporting categories covering smallholder procurement and land use change. Implementation timelines vary by jurisdiction but most are targeted for the 2027 reporting cycle.

item.title: Regional research consortium examines smallholder inclusion in Asian food supply chains
item.description: A consortium of five Southeast Asian universities has launched a three-year research programme examining smallholder inclusion in regional food supply chains. Early output is expected to inform both corporate procurement policy and regional regulatory discussions through 2028.

item.title: Singapore cultivated meat startup closes $85m Series C for lab-grown chicken
item.description: A Singapore-based cultivated meat company has raised eighty-five million dollars in Series C funding, with Temasek and GIC participating. The company plans regional expansion and scale-up of production facilities. No supply chain or ingredient sourcing strategy has been disclosed alongside the announcement.

item.title: Global plant-based protein market projected to reach $78 billion by 2030
item.description: Market research published this week projects the global plant-based protein market will reach seventy-eight billion dollars by 2030, driven primarily by North American and European demand. Asia-Pacific growth is expected to trail other regions, with analysts citing consumer preference and price sensitivity as key constraints.

item.title: Vertical farming startup opens pilot facility in Singapore industrial park
item.description: A vertical farming startup has opened a pilot facility in a Singapore industrial park, focusing on leafy greens for domestic restaurant supply. The pilot uses precision agriculture techniques and is supported by a government innovation grant.
